visits.note: The patient presents with symptoms of fatigue, polyuria, and polydipsia. The physical examination reveals a non-pitting edema of the lower extremities. The patient has a history of Type 2 Diabetes, Hypertension, and Hyperlipidemia. The plan is to optimize medication adherence and lifestyle modifications.

The patient returns with symptoms of worsening fatigue, shortness of breath, and increased edema. The physical examination reveals bilateral pitting edema of the lower extremities. An ECG shows evidence of left ventricular hypertrophy. The plan is to initiate a diuretic and adjust medications.

The patient returns with symptoms of worsening shortness of breath and orthopnea. The physical examination reveals bilateral pitting edema of the lower extremities and a 2+ S3 heart sound. The plan is to initiate spironolactone and optimize current medications.
